svg.text: Top-down view. Coloured lines = wires to solder on the underside (jumpers between holes). Components shown to approximate scale.

g.text: USB on LEFT — pin labels show the silkscreen as printed on the board

svg.text: Pin labels follow the DevKit-C v4 silkscreen. The four labels in blue are the GPIOs used here.

g.text: Wire colour legend (solder these on the back of the board)
Tip: build and test one channel end-to-end before populating the other three. Resistors are non-polarised; MOSFETs and the buck module are.
1× ESP32-WROOM-32 DevKit (30-pin) · 1× BUNTOR (or similar) 12V→3.3V buck converter · 1× 12V/≥3A power supply
4× IRLZ34N N-channel logic-level MOSFET (TO-220) · 4× 330Ω 1/4W resistor (R1–R4) · 4× 10kΩ 1/4W resistor (R5–R8)
5× 2-pin 5.08mm screw terminal block · 1× perfboard ~80×100mm · hookup wire (24 AWG or similar)
⚠ IRLZ34N pinout (looking at the FRONT face with the leads pointing down): pin 1 = Gate (G), pin 2 = Drain (D), pin 3 = Source (S). Double-check before soldering.
